transcript.clean_text: each and every stage of the Tour de France is available to watch live and On Demand on gcn Plus the Peloton gathered for another day a day with an opportunity for the sprinters the likes to follow pool and alpacinda kernick busy Jasper Philip to their main man and a bass fans who ready to say goodbye to the race as it left Spain and headed to France a day after the first French win Victor olafe in the green jersey Adam Yates leading the race and the race itself leaving Amore and on the way to Bayonne obayana still in El scalaria at The Basque Homeland but over the Border in France four categorized climbs to take on one intermediate Sprint and 193.5 kilometer to the distance no drama at the start there was though a very popular Rider at the front again a hat-trick of breakaways for Nielsen Palace king of the mountains looking for more King of the mountains points he was joined by 36 year old Frenchman but all the glory all the fandom all of the attention was on Nielsen Palace Victor Lafayette in the green points Jersey was looking for something else he managed to pick up as many points as he could behind in the intermediate Sprint it'll be Mass Peterson quicker than the rest would soon be back in the Peloton after that quick dart out and the day would continue as calm as it could be Nielsen Palace went over the fourth and final climb with maximum points and he'd soon give a fist bump to his mate in the Breakaway and begin to conserve energy after three tiring days could go on and opt to try and take the most combative Rider prize because a stage win well that would be a pipe dream across leaving Spain and heading into France through iRun into ondai and nouvella bishan himself would say goodbye to the Breakaway with 37 kilometers remaining all together and the nerves continuing to build for an expected first Sprint finish of this tour writers arriving in Bayonne for a big finale that finale was technical it was twisty it was tourney it was very very competitive Riders trains competing everywhere Mark Cavendish looking to make history with that 35th win perhaps and he rode fairly well he'd end up in the top ten but he would not take the win that will go to Jasper Phillips let out by Macho for the port but he'd have to wait after the Finish because he came around the final Corner the barriers started in what fanart looked like he might have been pushed out the way but on review nothing wrong for Jasper Phillips who celebrated before being forced to wait philipsa beating Phil barhaus to the line great work from alpacinda kerning the nervy weight came because of this post to keep your straight sprinting line but the Sprint itself went around the corner so Philip said it could be argued did in the end it seemed the correct decision to be made by the commissars despite the long deliberation so Philips are winning the stage ahead of bar house Ewan in third place Jacobson fourth fanart in fifth with Cavendish Peterson and Coco in a brilliant top ten another day in the yellow Jersey for Adam Yates UAM are at telling a perfect start a six second lead over teammate today pogacha and brother Simon Yates who rides for Jayco alula Victor Lafayette despite it being a Sprint day keeping the green jersey but level on points with Jasper Phillips and perhaps another expected Sprint day to come on day four and A4 will leave Dax and head to nogaho going directly east for 181.8 kilometers thank you and then back on itself one categorized climb nowhere near as much climbing on the day another interesting and highly competitive finish and you'll be able to see it all live and On Demand by watching gcn plus join us then foreign
